subtitle: The Bay-of-Bengal sea beach of Puri — one of India's first Blue Flag beaches, in one of Hinduism's four holiest cities, guarded by the hereditary Nulia lifeguards in their conical white hats.
spike_statement: One of India's first Blue Flag beaches, in the sacred Char Dham city of Puri, watched over by a century-old line of Nulia fishermen-lifeguards in conical white hats.

levers.photo_tone: golden sand and the gray-green Bay of Bengal under bright eastern light, sunrise pilgrim crowds, the white conical hats of the Nulia lifeguards, sand sculptures along the tideline
